title: thymus serpyllum f. serpens
intro: Thymus serpyllum f. serpens, also known as creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading variety of the thyme plant that is native to Europe and western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The health benefits of creeping thyme include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and even exhibit antifungal and antibacterial properties. Creeping thyme can be prepared as an herbal tea, added to food as a seasoning, or used in topical applications such as salves and ointments to utilize its medicinal properties.
